methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $81M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Wispr AI is a Series A-stage biotech & health company founded by Tanay Kothari that has raised $81M to date. Valuation was estimated using two applicable methods triangulated against a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the same sector. Key private peers include Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), Neko Health ($1.8B, Series B), Asimov ($800M, Series B), and Profluent ($600M, Series B).

Stage-progression methodology applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $81M total raised as a proxy valuation baseline, implying $162M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yielded a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring using a 6.1x P/S median across three biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) was not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue data.

These two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $138M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median estimate of $731M. The substantial spread reflects material uncertainty around stage timing and investor appetite relative to peer valuations. Revenue visibility and clinical progress remain critical sensitivities to narrowing this range.
